title: Listing Addressees — 'To:' Line Only
plain_language_rule: A multiple-address letter may list addressees using one of three methods based on the number of addressees and the nature of the correspondence. Use the 'To:' Line Only method when: (1) all addressees are entitled to a 'To:' line — no addressee requires a 'Via:' line; (2) there are only a few addressees (4 or fewer is the stated rule-of-thumb); and (3) the correspondence communicates information directly to the addressees (i.e., command-type correspondence). Under this method, all addressees are listed on the 'To:' line, a single letter goes to each addressee, and a 'Distribution:' line is not used. This format is one of three mutually exclusive methods — the alternative methods are 'Distribution:' Line Only (8-2b) and both 'To:' and 'Distribution:' Lines (8-2c).
source_text:
8-2. Listing Addressees.

One of three methods will be used to list addressees in a multiple-address letter. The method selected should be based on the number of addressees involved and the nature of the correspondence.

8-2a. "To:" Line Only.

When (1) all addressees are entitled to a "To:" line (i.e., no "Via:" addressees); (2) there are only a few addressees (4 or less is a good rule-of-thumb); and (3) the information is being communicated directly to the addressees (i.e., command-type correspondence); use the "To:" Line Only method.

Under this method, all addressees are listed on the "To:" line, with a single letter dispatched to each addressee. A "Distribution:" line is not used.
renderer_impact: When this format type is selected: (1) all addressees appear on the 'To:' line; (2) no 'Distribution:' line is rendered; (3) a separate copy of the letter is generated per addressee. The renderer must count addressees and ensure the count does not exceed the rule-of-thumb threshold. If conditions change (addressee count grows, Via addressees appear, or indirect communication is needed), the format must switch to an alternative method from 8-2b or 8-2c.
notes: Source text resolved 2026-05-11. The ≤4 threshold is described as a 'good rule-of-thumb,' not a hard limit — ambiguity preserved. 'Command-type correspondence' is not defined in this section; the renderer cannot enforce it algorithmically. Condition (1) depends on whether any addressee requires a via chain, which requires knowledge of the organizational hierarchy. This is a format-selection rule, not a layout-validation rule; no address-block fields are validated here. Related rules: C8-003 (Distribution: Line Only), C8-004 (both To: and Distribution:), C8-001 (general scope).
